title: Chapter 5 - Headings
description: Quick check on headings and hierarchy.

questions.prompt: Which heading should usually be used only once per page?

explanations.overview: <h1> is typically the main title of the page.

byOptionId.h1: Correct — <h1> is usually the single main title of a page.
byOptionId.h2: Incorrect — <h2> is for subsection titles, not usually the main page title.
byOptionId.h6: Incorrect — <h6> is a very low-level heading.

questions.prompt: If you have a main title and two subsections, a good order of tags is:

explanations.overview: Keep headings in logical descending order for clarity and accessibility.

byOptionId.a: Correct — a main <h1> followed by <h2> subsections is logical.
byOptionId.b: Incorrect — keep the main title at <h1>.
byOptionId.c: Incorrect — you need 2 equal-level headings (<h2> and <h2>).
byOptionId.d: Incorrect — this reverses the proper hierarchy. <h1> is big, and <h3> is small.
